title: isotrema involutum
intro: Isotrema involutum, also known as the 'Kunth's Isotrema' or 'Hairy Isotrema', is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Isotrema involutum are believed to include relief from fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Isotrema involutum typically involve its roots and leaves, which can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
